hitchens_question: When the Pentagon announces a "Cyber Mastery Incentive Pay" program while simultaneously admitting it cannot even build a proper data center, and Congress debates whether to let vital surveillance authorities lapse because they're squabbling over who gets to run the spy shop, one begins to wonder: Are we witnessing the decline of American imperial competence, or have we simply reached the stage where our military-industrial complex has become too bloated and bureaucratic to distinguish between actual security and profitable theater? Because if you can track grain smuggling to Libya but can't keep your own digital house in order, perhaps the barbarians aren't at the gates—they're running the IT department.
